name: Grade 1 Reading — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

lessons.title: Practice phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: Common Core State Standards for ELA
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Reading pathway.

units.title: Investigate: phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to phonics, high-frequency words, fluency, and story elements.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
